name: classify-legacy-changes
title: Classify Legacy Changes And Cleanup Noise
description: Classify old repo changes into historical direction, still-valid implementation base, and cleanup noise before any destructive cleanup work is attempted.
creator: shixiangliu
notes: Classification accepted as the cleanup-framing record for the current reset. In the original mono-workspace, root-level `.trellis` and `backend` were historical path-migration noise, while the app-layer paths became the active product roots. This extracted repo preserves the active roots as `.trellis`, `backend`, `frontend`, and `docs`. Cleanup stays separate from feature work, and this task remains unarchived as a live reference.

meta.goal: prepare safe cleanup decisions without mixing cleanup into product implementation tasks
round_1_summary: Historical direction and path-migration noise exists at repo root
Active implementation foundation is inside this repo plus repo docs
Cleanup should proceed in a dedicated follow-up instead of inside feature tasks
round_2_summary: .trellis/tasks/archive/* is valid active archival structure, not cleanup noise by default
Current backend and frontend modifications align with the active product path and should be preserved
Only the source-of-truth trio in repo docs should drive product direction; other docs remain supporting context
Repository entry instructions and Trellis instructions can coexist with different scope
